description: Repo-local agent harness with AGENTS.md, README, architecture, testing, quality, feature list, progress log, and active exec plan.
acceptance_steps: AGENTS.md links to architecture, testing, quality, active exec plan, progress log, and feature list.
README mentions every public CLI command and required env var.
Feature list is valid JSON.

description: Reusable Yandex LLM helper compatible with scripts/yandex_llm_example.py.
acceptance_steps: Request body matches Yandex Foundation Models completion shape.
Completion text extraction supports Yandex alternatives payload.

description: Analyze pages into structured opportunity JSON without guessing missing facts.
acceptance_steps: Invalid LLM JSON is rejected.
Missing fields are filled with safe defaults.
VK Dobro-like and SberVmeste-like fixtures are parsed.

description: Local workflow supports add-link, analyze, checklist, draft, and digest.
acceptance_steps: Local JSON integration flow works without external services.
Drafts use only FundWiki and mark missing facts.
Digest sorts overdue and urgent actions deterministically.

description: Telegram command handlers expose digest, add_link, checklist, draft, and status behavior.
acceptance_steps: Handlers return text replies without sending external messages in tests.
Status updates go through the store and activity log.

description: Harness includes the original fundraising-system intent and a staged roadmap for future agents beyond the MVP.
acceptance_steps: docs/ROADMAP.md captures the original task, product north star, MVP, and future agent phases.
AGENTS.md and README.md point future agents to docs/ROADMAP.md.
Domain docs refer to the roadmap instead of duplicating all future scope.

description: Harness requires detailed operator-facing service usage instructions to stay current.
acceptance_steps: docs/USAGE.md explains setup, env vars, local store, basic workflow, discovery, Telegram, Google Sheets, and human review.
AGENTS.md explicitly requires updating docs/USAGE.md when service usage changes.
README.md links to docs/USAGE.md.

description: Harness captures UI direction for a non-IT fundraiser before the MVP grows into a broader system.
acceptance_steps: docs/UI_STRATEGY.md identifies the primary non-IT user and UI principles.
docs/UI_STRATEGY.md outlines the first UI slice and future UI phases.
AGENTS.md, README.md, ROADMAP.md, and QUALITY.md point future agents to the UI strategy.

description: A non-IT operator can run the first verified workflow with a selectable store backend, diagnostics, and offline smoke test.
acceptance_steps: CLI supports --store-backend local|google and doctor.
Local smoke workflow exercises add-link, analyze, checklist, draft, and digest without external services.
README and docs/USAGE.md document doctor, BALANCE_STORE_BACKEND, --store-backend, and operator recipes.
docs/UI_STRATEGY.md ties the first web UI slice to the same operator workflow.

description: Local server-rendered web UI exposes the first non-IT operator workflow without external sends.
acceptance_steps: CLI supports web with --host and --port plus BALANCE_WEB_HOST and BALANCE_WEB_PORT.
Web UI renders dashboard, opportunity list, opportunity detail, checklist, draft, and local analysis form.
POST handlers add links and run heuristic analysis through existing services.
README, docs/USAGE.md, and docs/UI_STRATEGY.md document the local web UI.
scripts/dev_check.py includes a web render smoke check.

description: Web UI becomes a more comfortable operator workspace with review queue, safe record actions, notes, and checklist progress.
acceptance_steps: Dashboard shows priority groups for today, review, missing deadlines, and drafts with gaps.
Review queue shows unreviewed opportunities and hides fully reviewed accepted items.
Opportunity detail separates meaning, requirements, unknowns, evidence, checklist, and draft.
POST actions update status, review state, owner, notes, and checklist_done without external sends.
docs/UI_STRATEGY.md and docs/USAGE.md describe Phase 2 review/edit behavior and human-review boundaries.

description: Operator can maintain FundWiki passport facts, see application readiness blockers, and seed a safe local demo workflow.
acceptance_steps: Web UI shows and updates required FundWiki fields without external sends.
Opportunity detail shows readiness blockers for documents, deadline, missing info, FundWiki gaps, and low confidence.
CLI seed-demo creates a stable local training dataset without external services.
README, docs/USAGE.md, docs/UI_STRATEGY.md, and web smoke checks document the FundWiki/readiness workflow.

description: Operator can run a real application workflow through internal application stages, deadlines, notes, and first-run feedback without external sends.
acceptance_steps: Application records can be created from opportunities and keep opportunity_id linkage.
Web UI exposes /applications plus opportunity application actions for status, dates, owner, and notes.
Digest includes overdue response, reporting, recheck, and missing-owner application items.
First-run validation screen records operator observations in ActivityLog.
README, docs/USAGE.md, docs/UI_STRATEGY.md, and smoke checks document the guided application pipeline.

description: Operator can manage application response follow-up, reporting, application history, and first-run feedback statuses without external sends.
acceptance_steps: Application detail page shows linked opportunity, dates, next action, notes, response summary, reporting checklist, and activity history.
Response and reporting actions update only internal records and write ActivityLog entries.
Applications list links records and shows blockers for missing owner, overdue response, overdue report, and recheck-later.
First-run feedback entries have internal statuses that can be updated through the web UI.
README, docs/USAGE.md, docs/UI_STRATEGY.md, and smoke checks document the follow-up/reporting workflow.

description: Operator can run curated opportunity discovery from the local web UI and review new findings before any analysis or application work.
acceptance_steps: Web UI exposes /radar with curated queries, one-time custom query, safe limit, latest runs, and discovered opportunities.
DiscoveryService deduplicates URLs and records created vs existing counts in ActivityLog.
Discovery failures are logged without Yandex secrets and shown as operator-friendly run history.
CLI discover supports --query and --limit while preserving default discovery behavior.
README, docs/USAGE.md, docs/UI_STRATEGY.md, and smoke checks document the radar workflow.

description: Common local-first lead workspace for future fundraising directions beyond platform applications.
acceptance_steps: FundraisingLead is stored in local JSON and Google Sheets through the shared store interface.
CLI supports leads, lead-add, lead-show, and lead-status without external services.
Web UI exposes /leads and /leads/<id> with safe status, owner, and note actions.
Digest and review queue include leads that need owner, review, follow-up, or deadline attention.
README, docs/USAGE.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, and active exec plan document autonomous local-first roadmap development.

description: B2B partner agent for company leads, fit analysis, risk checks, and human-reviewed first-contact drafts.
acceptance_steps: B2B leads use the shared lead workspace.
B2B radar and drafts run with mocked external clients in tests.
Drafts are human-review only and use approved FundWiki and lead evidence.

description: Paid service offer workspace for corporate lectures, workshops, internships, and educational products.
acceptance_steps: Service offers are stored locally and can be reviewed in the web UI.
B2B drafts can reference selected approved offers.
Unknown prices or promises are marked instead of guessed.

description: Events and merch workflow for markets, materials, volunteer shifts, and post-event reporting.
acceptance_steps: Event leads use the shared lead workspace.
Event checklist covers application deadline, materials, volunteers, merch, costs, and post-report.
No inventory-heavy workflow is added before validation.

description: Blogger and ambassador workflow with ethics checks, reputation risks, and human-reviewed collaboration drafts.
acceptance_steps: Blogger leads use the shared lead workspace.
Risk checklist flags stigma, pressure, consent, and reputation issues.
Templates avoid stigmatizing language.

description: Private donor campaign workspace for segments, gratitude, impact digest, and reactivation drafts without storing personal donor data.
acceptance_steps: Donor campaigns store segment-level records only.
Drafts are human-review only and avoid manipulative language.
No personal donor data is required for v1.

description: Unified dashboard, digest, and review queue across platforms, applications, leads, offers, events, bloggers, and donor campaigns.
acceptance_steps: Dashboard groups urgent work across all implemented agent modules.
Review queue uses consistent human-review boundaries.
Digest remains deterministic in tests.

description: Final real-world validation, integration hardening, and UI/deployment decisions after the roadmap skeleton is implemented.
acceptance_steps: Manual real-world smoke covers all implemented agent modules.
Findings are recorded before UI framework, auth, deployment, or real-send integrations are chosen.
External integrations remain optional and documented.
